title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte from Somerville, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his nephews with Down syndrome.
Ponte's twin nephews, Sebastian and Zachary, were diagnosed with Down syndrome shortly after birth and received support from MDSC.
Ponte's run aims to honor Sebastian's memory and support Zachary's future by raising awareness for Down syndrome families.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston firefighter and National Guard Major Mark Garcia was injured in an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait.
The strike in Kuwait resulted in the deaths of six U.S. soldiers; Major Garcia is recovering in Germany.
Garcia serves with Boston's Engine 50 and is praised by the Boston Firefighters IAFF Local 718 for his dedication.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The retrial of the Phan brothers for a 2020 Lowell murder is delayed due to complications involving a State Police investigator.
Sgt. Scott Quigley, the lead investigator, allegedly caused a fatal crash while driving drunk in 2023, prompting trial delays.
Justice Barry-Smith ruled the April 27 trial date unrealistic, with a status conference set for June and bail hearing scheduled.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman has posted a .906 save percentage this season, an improvement from last year's .892 percentage.
The Boston Bruins' playoff chances hinge on Swayman's performance, as they hold the second Wild Card spot in the East.
Despite defensive struggles, Swayman's exceptional play has resulted in a current 13-game win streak at TD Garden.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police released body camera footage from a private Patriots party at restaurant Estella on January 27.
Officers noted violations including smoking, unauthorized adult entertainment, and after-hours drinking at the Boston restaurant.
The Boston Licensing Board suspended Estella's license for three days, blaming owner Helder George Brandao and the players.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots signed safety Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million contract after his All-Pro season with the Bears.
Byard, known for durability, hasn't missed an NFL game in 10 seasons, notably playing through numerous injuries.
Kevin Byard's college career at Middle Tennessee State included 318 tackles and 19 interceptions, leading to his jersey being retired.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt resentenced to 14-17 years for 2011 Dorchester shooting, eligible for parole due to time served.
Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court overturned Watt's previous convictions, citing denial of right to counsel.
Victims Jaivon Blake and Kimoni Elliot were targeted in gang-related shooting, Blake was killed and Elliot seriously injured.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams introduces 'Our City. Our Beer.' pack with brews inspired by Boston athletes White, Crochet, Edelman, and Chára.
The pack features four distinct beers: White Ale, Crochet's Pale Ale, Julian Edel-Brau Lager, and Big Z Pilsner.
Public launch event at Banners Kitchen & Tap on April 4 will include appearances by the featured athletes.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston Marathon Race Director Dave McGillivray will run his 54th marathon with his children Max, Luke, and Elle McGillivray.
Retired astronaut Sunita Williams to join McGillivray in the 2026 Boston Marathon as part of the Finish Strong Foundation's team.
Family members will dedicate their runs to charitable causes, honoring Susan Hurley and supporting various philanthropic efforts.

title: Jayson Tatum, Derrick White sound off on ‘ridiculous’ Jaylen Brown ejection
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected before halftime in the Celtics' loss to San Antonio, causing frustration among teammates.
Jayson Tatum criticized the referee's decision, noting it was a national TV game with major stars involved.
Derrick White agreed the first technical was justified but called the second ejection during a high-stakes game excessive.
